genetic_lineage.overview: Poulsard is indigenous to the Jura with no confirmed parentage. DNA studies suggest it is unrelated to other major French varieties.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to the Jura.

siblings.name: Trousseau
siblings.notes: Often blended together in Jura reds for complementary characteristics.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Poulsard's genetic isolation confirms its status as a founding variety of Jura viticulture. The variety's unique characteristics have no parallel in other French wine regions.

clones.name: Arbois Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional Jura selections. Pale color with delicate red fruit aromatics.

clones.name: Pupillin Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections from the village known as 'capitale du Ploussard'. Maximum aromatic intensity.

clones.name: Clone 743
clones.characteristics: Certified clone with good disease resistance and consistent quality.

clones.name: Old Vine Selection
clones.characteristics: Massal selections from centenarian vines. Exceptional concentration despite pale color.

terroir.effect: The dominant soil of the Jura. Heavy clay-rich marl produces wines with mineral depth and finesse. Retains water well in dry years.

wine_character.aromatics: Sour cherry, red currant, rose, mineral

terroir.effect: Lighter marl found at higher sites. Produces more elegant wines with floral notes.
Found on slopes. Better drainage produces more structured wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Thin skins make it highly susceptible to grey rot.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Can devastate crops in wet years

susceptibilities.notes: Can suffer poor fruit set in cool, wet springs.

resistances.notes: Reasonable frost tolerance but late bud break helps avoid spring frost.

disease_profile.general_notes: Poulsard is a challenging variety to grow due to its thin skins and disease susceptibility. The variety ripens mid-season and produces wines of remarkable delicacy. Extended maceration can extract more color but risks bitterness. Many producers now favor short macerations or even carbonic methods to preserve freshness.

regional_expressions.style_summary: The heart of Poulsard production, where the variety can be bottled alone or blended. Wines are extremely pale, often mistaken for rosé, with delicate red fruit aromatics and high acidity. The natural wine movement has brought international attention to these distinctive wines.

typical_character.aromatics: Sour cherry, red currant, rose, earth, mineral

regional_expressions.style_summary: The village of Pupillin is known as the 'capitale du Ploussard' (local name for Poulsard). Wines often show more intensity and complexity than standard Arbois, with the village terroir adding distinctiveness.

typical_character.aromatics: Sour cherry, red currant, rose, spice, mineral

regional_expressions.style_summary: The broader Jura appellation also produces Poulsard in similar styles. Often blended with Trousseau and Pinot Noir to add color and structure. Good value alternative to Arbois.
